sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, is a 7/10 in terms of pain severity due to a car accident one week ago, where he hit the back of his head and neck, causing his head to jerk forward.
The pain is located in the back of his neck and worsens with movement.
He has tried ibuprofen with minimal relief.
The pain has been consistent over the past week, with occasional worsening with movement.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing football in the past but denies any previous neck issues.
Review of systems: The patient denies any headaches, hearing problems, or visual disturbances.
Current medications: The patient has not mentioned any current medications.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, severe positive pain with extension, and positive pain with rotation.
He is able to turn his neck from side to side with pain, but is unable to touch his ear to his shoulder due to pain.
Test Results: The patient's X-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain due to whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to be conservative with neck movement and avoid straining his neck further.
I also ordered an MRI to rule out any potential nerve impingement.
I will refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: I will refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment.
